summary: Problem 4 asks for a finite free probability analog of the Blachman-Stam Fisher information inequality, specifically the subharmonicity (superadditivity of the reciprocal) of 1/Phi_n under finite free convolution. While 40 of 45 issues are resolved, 5 critical open gaps remain that block a complete proof, most notably an unproven equivalence between the defining coefficient formula and the Walsh averaging formula, plus unverified conditions in the simple-roots reduction argument. The current best proof still carries 100 open issues at the model level, and the problem's 16% solvability score reflects its research-level difficulty.
problems: Foundational gap: the equivalence between the bilinear coefficient formula (c_k = sum_{i+j=k} ...) defining finite free convolution and the eq:walsh averaging formula is unproven, undermining the entire framework the proof relies on.
Reduction to simple roots is incomplete (Gap 3): T_eps^2(p boxplus_n q) is not verified to have simple roots, so the limiting argument's hypotheses are not fully established.
Step C inversion of the lower-semicontinuity inequality (Gap 4) fails to handle the K=0 boundary case, leaving the deduction from liminf K >= K incomplete.
Variance additivity (Prop. prop:props (i)) proof is incomplete: Var(gamma) = Var(alpha) + Var(beta) under Psi is asserted but not fully justified.
The designated best proof attempt still reports 100 open issues, indicating the consolidated proof artifact is far from publication-ready despite many resolved sub-issues.
highlights: 40 of 45 issues are resolved, including several deep ones: the Hessian identity JJ* vs J*J dimensional error (Lemma 3), the missing 1/n! normalization in the Walsh formula, and the heat-flow commutativity with differential operators.
The factor-of-2 score-derivative concern in Heat Flow Lemma 1 was investigated, numerically verified, and confirmed correct, strengthening confidence in the heat-flow machinery.
Commutativity claims (Lemma 2(iii)) were upgraded from one-line citations to self-contained proofs, improving rigor and self-sufficiency.
The limiting argument from simple roots to the general case and the implicit differentiation conflation in Lemma 1 have both been rigorously fixed.

suggested_todos.title: Prove the equivalence of the bilinear coefficient formula and the eq:walsh averaging formula (foundational blocker)
Verify simple roots of T_eps^2(p boxplus_n q) in the reduction argument (Gap 3)
Handle the K=0 boundary case in the Step C LSC inequality inversion (Gap 4)
Complete the variance additivity proof for Prop. prop:props (i) including all cross-term cancellations
Consolidate all resolved sub-issues into a single clean proof artifact and re-audit to drive down the 100 open issues on the best proof
